entries.direction_title: Admissions and intake-equity regulation (keep structure, fix the gate)
entries.rationale: T1 directly regulates the admission mechanism (exam design, SES-weighting, interview cap) for existing 6/8-year tracks while leaving the track structure itself unchanged, matching S1's 'keep structure, fix the gate' framing.

entries.direction_title: Supply-side contraction of the selective track (shrink the gate, not the rules)
entries.rationale: T2 directly implements supply-side contraction via capacity caps and score thresholds in the state sector, matching S2's shrink-the-gate approach; T6 extends the contraction logic to prevent segregation-generating expansion across all maintainer types, closing the gap T2 leaves open for church/private tracks, and is included here as a complementary supply-side lever operating through administrative approval rather than admission-criteria change.

entries.direction_title: Structural delay of the selection age (Finnish/Polish model)
entries.rationale: T3 is the structural-delay proposal explicitly raising the statutory tracking age to 14+, phasing out 6/8-year entry points into a unified comprehensive lower-secondary phase bundled with anti-sorting catchment rules, directly matching the Finnish/Polish model described in S3.

entries.direction_title: Non-structural compensatory reform (leave selection age unchanged)
entries.rationale: T4 leaves the 10-12 selection age and track structure entirely untouched and instead pursues equity via targeted (TEIP-style) funding, curriculum clarity, and retention reduction, directly matching S4's Portugal-style non-structural compensatory reform.

T5 is the explicit no-new-policy counterfactual in which demographic decline and statutory class-size minimums mechanically force passive network consolidation and shrinkage of the selective track's absolute size, matching S5's status-quo baseline function against which T1-T4 (and T6) are measured.
